name: BESE: Base-Efficient Subword Encoding — Novel 38-Token Structured Alphabet + BPE
author: Omer Bese
val_bpb_note: Measured on 1/80th of training data (12.6M tokens vs 1B baseline). See README for analysis.
summary: Novel two-layer tokenizer: 38-token linguistically structured alphabet + 250 BPE merges = 288 vocab. Saves ~295KB of embedding parameters vs 1024-vocab baseline, enough for 2-3 extra transformer layers.
notes: Novel tokenizer submission — non-record track. High BPB is due to 80x data starvation, not tokenizer flaw. Train loss drops normally (5.69->1.22). Full-data run pending compute credits.
